subject: Mathematics
title: The Rabbit Problem
description: A quick win: understand The Rabbit Problem.

hook.text: In 1202, Fibonacci posed a puzzle: how fast do rabbits multiply if each pair breeds monthly?

buildup.text: Month 1: 1 pair. Month 2: 1 pair. Month 3: 2 pairs. Month 4: 3. Month 5: 5. Month 6: 8.

discovery.text: Each month's total equals the sum of the two months before: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21.

twist.text: Fibonacci posed this puzzle in Liber Abaci to promote Hindu-Arabic numerals—not to study rabbits.

climax.text: The Fibonacci sequence became the most famous in all of mathematics, appearing everywhere in nature.

punchline.text: A rabbit riddle produced a sequence that spans all of science.

quiz.question: What rule generates the Fibonacci sequence?
options: Each number is the sum of the two before it
Each number doubles the previous one
